quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: In Hungary, people traditionally celebrate their name day rather than their birthday! Each day of the year is associated with one or more names, and Ildikó's name day is celebrated on November 17th. It's a cherished tradition where friends and family offer flowers and small gifts.

short_read.title: The Secret Social Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jeweled birds are more social than we thought, remembering individual humans and other hummingbirds for years. Scientists are discovering they use surprisingly complex communication and even teach their young which flowers to visit.

medium_read.title: How Trees Talk to Each Other
medium_read.teaser: Deep beneath the forest floor, trees communicate through an underground network of fungi, sharing nutrients and even warning each other of danger. This 'wood wide web' reveals that forests are more like communities than we ever imagined.

long_read.title: The Journey of the Oregon Trail: America's Great Migration West
long_read.teaser: Between 1840 and 1869, over 400,000 pioneers made the perilous 2,000-mile journey westward seeking new lives. This fascinating account brings to life their courage, challenges, and the remarkable communities they built along the way.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Late May is a wonderful time to spot Olympic black-tailed deer at Hurricane Ridge, as does are nursing their spotted fawns in the high meadows. These deer are unique to the Pacific Northwest and are smaller than their eastern cousins. The meadows are also bursting with wildflowers right now, making it a particularly magical time to visit.

puzzle_1.question: The Strait of Juan de Fuca, which you can see from Port Angeles, is about ___ miles wide at its narrowest point.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! It's amazing to think that Canada is just 11 miles across the water from Port Angeles.
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The Strait is actually about 11 miles wide at its narrowest point — you can often see across to Vancouver Island on clear days!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The Strait is actually about 11 miles wide at its narrowest point between Port Angeles and Victoria. On a clear day, you really can see across!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and is named after a Greek sailor who explored the area in 1592. It's an important shipping channel and home to orcas, seals, and many seabirds.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for these delicate creatures, isn't it?
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — such a lovely, musical word! (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language unrelated to other European languages — it's part of the Finno-Ugric family. Many Hungarian words are wonderfully expressive and poetic, especially nature words.

puzzle_3.question: What do gardeners call the last expected frost date in spring?
puzzle_3.correct_response: That's right! For Port Angeles, the 'Safe Date' is typically mid to late April — much earlier than many other places thanks to your mild coastal climate!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Gardeners call it the 'Safe Date' — the day after which it's generally safe to plant tender vegetables and flowers. In Port Angeles, that's usually mid to late April thanks to the mild marine climate!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Port Angeles enjoys a temperate maritime climate with mild winters, making it perfect for year-round gardening. The Olympic rain shadow also means the area gets less rain than Seattle!

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
